texto: Why should I be frightened of dying? I did not know what death truly was; no one did. Who had made dying a bad word? Yes, it was universally considered awful—unwanted, painful, feared—because when it happened it stopped us from moving and being, and we interpreted that as if something had ended. But what if it were actually a beautiful experience? What if, with death, something actually began instead?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Charles Novacek: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de enfrentar incertezas com ação. O trecho “Why should I be frightened of dying? I did not know what death truly was; no one did. Who had mad…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, czech-resistance, memoir pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e termino.
